Container.Name: nebularr-postgres
Container.Overview:
PostgreSQL for Nebularr (same role as [i]docker-compose.yml[/i] [i]postgres[/i] service).
[b]Before first install:[/b] - Docker → Networks → add a custom bridge network named [b]nebularr[/b] (must match [i]Network[/i] above).
[b]Arrapp role:[/b] No host init scripts are required. Start the Nebularr app after Postgres is healthy, open the Web UI setup wizard, and use the [b]Database[/b] step (or the bootstrap API) to create the [i]arrapp[/i] role and persist its connection string on the app volume.
[b]Start order:[/b] Start this container before Nebularr.

Config.Name: Postgres TCP (optional)
Config.Description: Publish Postgres for host/backup tools. Nebularr only needs it on the Docker network.

Config.Name: POSTGRES_USER
Config.Description: Superuser created by the official Postgres image.
